title: Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia: Diagnosis and Management
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of BPH and LUTS
Perform appropriate evaluation using validated tools
Select appropriate medical and surgical therapies

sections.title: Pathophysiology
sections.content:
**What is BPH?**
Benign proliferation of prostatic stromal and epithelial cells in the transition zone, causing bladder outlet obstruction.

**Two Components of Obstruction:**
• **Static:** Physical enlargement of prostate
• **Dynamic:** Smooth muscle tone (α1-receptors)

**Prevalence by Age:**
• 50s: 50%
• 60s: 60%
• 70s: 70%
• 80s: 80%

**Key Concept:** Prostate size does NOT correlate with symptom severity!

sections.title: Clinical Presentation - LUTS
sections.content:
**Storage Symptoms (Irritative):**
• Urgency
• Frequency
• Nocturia
• Urge incontinence

**Voiding Symptoms (Obstructive):**
• Hesitancy
• Weak stream
• Intermittency
• Straining
• Incomplete emptying
• Post-void dribbling

**AUA Symptom Score (IPSS):**
• 0-7: Mild
• 8-19: Moderate
• 20-35: Severe
• Includes quality of life question

sections.title: Evaluation
sections.content:
**History:**
• IPSS/AUA symptom score
• Medical history (DM, neurologic disease)
• Medications (anticholinergics, decongestants)
• Fluid intake patterns

**Physical Exam:**
• DRE: Size, consistency, nodules
• Abdominal exam for bladder distension

**Laboratory:**
• Urinalysis (rule out UTI, hematuria)
• PSA (optional, discuss with patient)
• Creatinine (if retention suspected)

**Post-Void Residual:**
• >200mL suggests significant retention
• Measured by ultrasound or catheterization

sections.title: Medical Therapy
sections.content:
**Alpha-1 Blockers (First-line):**
• Tamsulosin, alfuzosin, silodosin
• Relax prostatic smooth muscle (dynamic component)
• Rapid onset (days to weeks)
• Side effects: Orthostatic hypotension, dizziness, retrograde ejaculation
• Intraoperative Floppy Iris Syndrome (IFIS) - inform ophthalmologist

**5-Alpha Reductase Inhibitors:**
• Finasteride, dutasteride
• Block testosterone→DHT conversion
• Reduce prostate size (static component)
• Slow onset (3-6 months)
• Side effects: Decreased libido, ED, gynecomastia
• Reduces PSA by 50% (adjust interpretation)

**Combination Therapy:**
• Alpha-blocker + 5-ARI for large prostates (>30g)
• More effective than monotherapy
• MTOPS and CombAT trials

sections.title: Other Medical Options
sections.content:
**PDE5 Inhibitors:**
• Tadalafil 5mg daily
• indicated for BPH/LUTS
• Good option if concurrent ED

**Anticholinergics:**
• For predominant storage symptoms
• Use cautiously - can worsen retention
• Monitor PVR

**Beta-3 Agonists:**
• Mirabegron
• Alternative for storage symptoms
• Lower retention risk than anticholinergics

sections.title: Surgical Options
sections.content:
**Indications for Surgery:**
• Refractory urinary retention
• Recurrent UTIs
• Bladder stones
• Renal insufficiency from obstruction
• Recurrent gross hematuria
• Failed medical therapy

**Procedures:**
• **TURP:** Gold standard, prostates 30-80g
• **Laser procedures:** HoLEP, PVP (GreenLight)
• **Simple prostatectomy:** >80-100g prostates
• **Minimally invasive:** UroLift, Rezum, Aquablation

**TURP Syndrome:**
• Hyponatremia from irrigation fluid absorption
• Confusion, nausea, visual changes
• Treat with hypertonic saline

sections.title: Complications of Untreated BPH
sections.content:
**Acute Urinary Retention:**
• Sudden inability to void
• Painful bladder distension
• Treatment: Catheterization, alpha-blocker, trial of void

**Chronic Retention:**
• May be painless
• Overflow incontinence
• Hydronephrosis and renal insufficiency

**Other Complications:**
• Recurrent UTIs
• Bladder stones
• Bladder decompensation
• Gross hematuria

keyTakeaways: BPH has static (size) and dynamic (tone) components
Prostate size does NOT correlate with symptom severity
5-ARIs reduce PSA by 50% - double the value for interpretation
Acute urinary retention is an indication for surgical referral

references.citation: McVary KT et al. Update on AUA Guideline on BPH. J Urol. 2011;185:1793-1803

reviewQuestions.question: A 65-year-old man with BPH on finasteride has a PSA of 2.5 ng/mL. What is his estimated true PSA?
reviewQuestions.explanation: 5-alpha reductase inhibitors reduce PSA by approximately 50%. To interpret PSA in patients on finasteride or dutasteride, double the measured value. His estimated true PSA is 5.0 ng/mL, which warrants further evaluation.

reviewQuestions.question: A 58-year-old man presents with moderate LUTS (IPSS 15) and a prostate estimated at 25g on DRE. Which medication is most appropriate for initial monotherapy?
reviewQuestions.explanation: For a smaller prostate (<30-40g), alpha-blockers alone are first-line therapy. 5-ARIs are most beneficial for larger prostates. Combination therapy is reserved for prostates >30g. Tamsulosin will provide rapid symptom relief.

reviewQuestions.question: Which of the following is an absolute indication for surgical intervention in BPH?
options: Recurrent urinary retention despite medical therapy
reviewQuestions.explanation: Recurrent urinary retention despite medical therapy is an absolute indication for surgery. Other absolute indications include renal insufficiency from obstruction, bladder stones, and recurrent UTIs due to BPH. Symptom severity and prostate size alone are relative indications.

mcqs.stem: Which of the following is the MOST important initial step in evaluating a patient with suspected bph?

options.text: Obtain a thorough history and physical examination
options.explanation: A comprehensive history and physical examination is the foundation of clinical reasoning in bph. It guides all subsequent diagnostic and management decisions.
Treatment should generally follow an appropriate diagnostic evaluation unless the patient is critically unstable.

options.text: Consult a specialist before any workup
options.explanation: Initial evaluation should be performed by the treating physician; specialist consultation follows based on findings.
Advanced imaging may be indicated but should be guided by clinical findings, not ordered reflexively.

correct.reinforcement: Correct! Obtain a thorough history and physical examination.
correct.deeperWhy: Understanding this concept is foundational for clinical reasoning in this domain.
correct.clinicalPearl: Remember: Obtain a thorough history and physical examination

coachDx.misconception: Initial evaluation should be performed by the treating physician; specialist consultation follows based on findings.
coachDx.guidance: Review the key features that distinguish Obtain a thorough history and physical examination from Consult a specialist before any workup.
coachDx.bridgeTo: The key distinction involves Obtain a thorough history and physical examination.

mcqs.stem: A patient presents with classic features of bph. Which clinical reasoning principle is MOST important in avoiding diagnostic error?

options.text: Search satisficing - stop looking after the first diagnosis
options.explanation: Premature closure (stopping the diagnostic process too early) is a common cognitive error that leads to missed diagnoses.
Systematic consideration of differential diagnoses, including must-not-miss conditions, is essential for avoiding premature closure and anchoring bias.
While pattern recognition (System 1 thinking) is useful, it should be balanced with analytical reasoning (System 2) to avoid cognitive biases.

options.text: Order every available test to rule out all possibilities
options.explanation: Indiscriminate testing leads to false positives, unnecessary procedures, and increased cost without improving diagnostic accuracy.

correct.reinforcement: Correct! Consider the differential diagnosis systematically.
correct.deeperWhy: Understanding this concept is foundational for clinical reasoning in this domain.
correct.clinicalPearl: Remember: Consider the differential diagnosis systematically

coachDx.misconception: Indiscriminate testing leads to false positives, unnecessary procedures, and increased cost without improving diagnostic accuracy.
coachDx.guidance: Review the key features that distinguish Consider the differential diagnosis systematically from Order every available test to rule out all possibilities.
coachDx.bridgeTo: The key distinction involves Consider the differential diagnosis systematically.

mcqs.stem: When managing bph, which approach best supports evidence-based practice?

options.text: Follow institutional protocols regardless of patient factors
options.explanation: Evidence-based practice integrates best evidence with clinical expertise AND patient values/circumstances.

options.text: Integrate current best evidence with clinical expertise and patient values
options.explanation: Evidence-based medicine is the conscientious integration of best research evidence, clinical expertise, and patient values in making clinical decisions.

options.text: Always defer to the most recent published study
options.explanation: Individual studies should be appraised for quality and relevance; systematic reviews and guidelines generally provide stronger evidence.

options.text: Base decisions solely on clinical experience
options.explanation: Clinical experience is valuable but can introduce bias; it should be combined with current evidence.

correct.reinforcement: Correct! Integrate current best evidence with clinical expertise and patient values.
correct.deeperWhy: Understanding this concept is foundational for clinical reasoning in this domain.
correct.clinicalPearl: Remember: Integrate current best evidence with clinical expertise and patient values

coachDx.misconception: Individual studies should be appraised for quality and relevance; systematic reviews and guidelines generally provide stronger evidence.
coachDx.guidance: Review the key features that distinguish Integrate current best evidence with clinical expertise and patient values from Always defer to the most recent published study.
coachDx.bridgeTo: The key distinction involves Integrate current best evidence with clinical expertise and patient values.
